q: Correct the underlined part: 'Have you eaten' a lot of sweets when you were a child?
a: Did you eat a lot of sweets when you were a child?

q: Correct the underlined part: 'I've bought' a new car last week.
a: I bought a new car last week.

q: Correct the underlined part: Where 'have you been' yesterday evening?
a: Where were you yesterday evening?

q: Correct the underlined part: Maria 'has left' school in 1999.
a: Maria left school in 1999.

q: Correct the underlined part: When 'has this bridge been' built?
a: When was this bridge built?

q: Make a sentence: (the weather / be / cold / recently)
a: The weather has been cold recently.

q: Make a sentence: (it / cold / last week)
a: It was cold last week.

q: Make a sentence: (I / not / eat / any fruit yesterday)
a: I did not eat any fruit yesterday.

q: Make a sentence: (I / not / eat / any fruit today)
a: I have not eaten any fruit today.

q: Make a sentence: (Emily / earn / a lot of money / this year)
a: Emily has earned a lot of money this year.

q: Make a sentence: (she / not / earn / so much / last year)
a: She did not earn so much last year.

q: Make a question: (you / have / a holiday recently?)
a: Have you had a holiday recently?

q: Put the verb in the correct form: I (not / be) to Canada, but I'd like to go there.
a: I have not been to Canada, but I'd like to go there.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: Paul and Lucy (arrive) about ten minutes ago.
a: Paul and Lucy arrived about ten minutes ago.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: I'm tired. I (not / sleep) well last night.
a: I'm tired. I did not sleep well last night.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: There (be) a bus drivers' strike last week.
a: There was a bus drivers' strike last week.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: Edward (work) in a bank for 15 years. Then he (give) it up.
a: Edward worked in a bank for 15 years. Then he gave it up.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: Mary lives in Dublin. She (live) there all her life.
a: Mary lives in Dublin. She has lived there all her life.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: My grandfather (die) before I was born.
a: My grandfather died before I was born.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: It's nearly lunchtime, and I (not / see) Martin all morning.
a: It's nearly lunchtime, and I have not seen Martin all morning.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: (you / go) to the cinema last night?
a: Did you go to the cinema last night?

q: Put the verb in the correct form: It (be) very warm here since we arrived.
a: It has been very warm here since we arrived.

q: Put the verb in the correct form: How long (you / live) in Boston?
a: How long have you lived in Boston?

q: Put the verb in the correct form: Where (you / live) before that?
a: Where did you live before that?

q: Put the verb in the correct form: How long (you / live) in Chicago?
a: How long did you live in Chicago?
